title: Bias, variance, and the generalization gap
description: Underfitting is high bias (model too simple), overfitting is high variance (model memorizes); the goal is the balance that minimizes test error.
body: Use when reasoning about why a model fails to generalize. High bias shows as poor training AND validation performance (too little capacity); high variance shows as excellent training but poor validation (memorization). The train/validation gap measures variance; the training error floor measures bias. Do not fix high variance by adding capacity or high bias by regularizing. Actions: read both curves, identify the regime, and apply the matching remedy (more capacity/features vs more data/regularization). Validation signals include a shrinking gap with held validation accuracy. Risk boundary: misreading the regime makes the model worse; confirm with both curves before acting.
